page: Microsoft Azure D8ns_v6 is a network-optimized virtual machine belonging to the Dnv6 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 4 physical Intel Xeon 8573C cores with 2 threads per core. Operating at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz, the processor includes 260 MB of L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for tile and INT8 operations. The instance is configured with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding 4.0 GB per core, and does not include local storage or onboard GPUs, relying instead on Premium Storage capabilities for remote disk volumes.

Benchmark evaluations place the D8ns_v6 in the average performance tier for most standard operations, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1900.68 and a multi-core score of 7619.12. Memory bandwidth tests show 591.47 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 64.69 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. The server demonstrates strong single-threaded performance in gzip compression at 30.39 GB/sec, placing it in the top 25% of tested hardware. For lightweight machine learning tasks, it achieves a strong text generation speed of 279.99 tokens/sec using a small 135M model, though performance drops to a weaker 8.08 tokens/sec when executing text generation on a medium 7B model.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the dedicated Intel Xeon hardware profile and balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio deliver reliable, non-burstable compute resources suitable for production environments without the premium cost of memory-optimized or GPU-accelerated tiers. The lack of local storage requires external storage provisioning, which should be factored into architectural planning. This server is well-suited for general-purpose workloads, network-optimized applications, static web serving, and database operations that benefit from dedicated CPU allocation and high clock speeds.
description: Microsoft Azure D8ns_v6 is a network-optimized, general-purpose virtual machine powered by an 8-vCPU Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 32.0 GB of RAM, 260 MB of L3 cache, and dedicated CPU allocation with support for AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX). This instance does not include local storage or GPUs, relying on Premium Storage capabilities for remote disk attachment. Benchmarks indicate average multi-core CPU and database performance, alongside strong single-threaded gzip compression and lightweight LLM text generation. The D8ns_v6 is designed for workloads requiring stable compute performance, such as static web serving, network-optimized applications, and general-purpose database operations.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D8ns_v6 is an 8-vCPU network-optimized virtual machine featuring an Intel Xeon 8573C processor, 32 GB of RAM, and Premium Storage capability, designed for stable general-purpose workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D8ns_v6 is a network-optimized virtual machine with 8 vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor for general-purpose workloads.
tagline: A dedicated eight-vCPU virtual machine featuring high-frequency Intel Xeon processing and balanced memory for network-optimized general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an 8-vCPU Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz
Equipped with 32.0 GB of RAM providing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-memory ratio
Supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and Intel AMX
Features Premium Storage capability with no local storage or onboard GPUs
Delivers strong single-threaded gzip compression and lightweight LLM text generation
Optimized for static web serving, network tasks, and general-purpose databases
